curriculum.name: Felipe Conceição
curriculum.title: Creative Director, motion branding and creative editing
curriculum.subtitle: Eight years translating brand strategy into rhythm, light and image — from generative pipelines to projection mapping.
curriculum.profileParagraph1: I've spent eight years working in the space where design decisions become timing decisions — because timing is what the audience actually remembers. My background in music trained that instinct before motion design gave it a medium.
curriculum.profileParagraph2: Today I apply it across industries, formats, and scales: building brands that don't just look right, but land at the right moment.

items: Systems lead for modular motion pipelines (digital/retail).
Brand strategy alignment for high-ticket positioning.
Strategic brands: Suzano, O Boticário, Colcci, FG and Embraed.
Focused on high-ticket events, blending painting and video art.
Owned post-production pipeline for aesthetic excellence.
Broadcast / Digital Signage assets for automotive, financial and corporate sector.
Motion Design for Live Streams and Advertising
Full-cycle video production for a high-volume online education platform: from course direction and capture to final edit and delivery.
— Extended scope into brand communications — producing advertising campaigns and internal materials alongside core course content.

curriculum.contactText: The system is ready. Looking for a full-time creative direction or motion design role — remote, with teams who move fast and think deep.

coverLetter.opening: I’m excited to apply for the Senior Motion / Video Designer role at Flo. With a strong background in motion design and video editing, I’m drawn to the opportunity to contribute to a mission-led product that combines craft, speed, and meaningful impact at scale.
coverLetter.body1: I bring experience creating motion graphics, editing thumbstopping short-form content, and adapting visuals for fast-moving campaign needs across digital channels. I’m comfortable working in high-output environments, collaborating with cross-functional teams, and producing assets that support both brand storytelling and performance goals.
coverLetter.body2: What sets me apart is my ability to balance creative quality with structured execution. I’m used to working with brand systems, iterating quickly, and using AI-enabled workflows where they genuinely improve speed, exploration, and production efficiency without compromising visual clarity or consistency.
coverLetter.closing: Flo stands out as a company that combines scale, purpose, and product thinking in a way that feels especially relevant right now. I’d value the chance to contribute to a team that moves fast, cares deeply about craft, and is building creative work that supports a global health mission with real impact.
coverLetter.signoff: I’d be glad to share more of my work and creative approach through my portfolio: https://pelimotion.vercel.app
coverLetter.subtitle: Creative Director, motion branding and artistic direction.
coverLetter.subtitle_pt: Diretor Criativo, motion branding e direção artística.

sections.title: OPENING
sections.body: I'm Felipe Conceição â Creative Director and motion designer behind Pelimotion Hub in Rio de Janeiro. For eight years I've been building visual systems for brands across Latin America and beyond: from full motion branding campaigns to modular asset libraries that marketing teams keep using long after launch day.

sections.title: WHAT I BRING
sections.body: My work lives at the intersection of strategy and execution. I concept, direct, and deliver — in After Effects, Illustrator, Premiere, and Figma — without needing a layer of translation between creative thinking and the timeline. I've produced CGI/motion content for BMW, Subaru, Hyundai, and Motul, and led the full motion branding for a brand relaunch that included both the launch film and a complete Motion Media Kit for ongoing marketing use.

sections.title: WHY DIFFERENT
sections.body: I approach motion the way a musician approaches rhythm — timing, weight, silence. That foundation, combined with AI-integrated workflows and a minimalist design philosophy, means I move fast without losing craft. Fluent in async-first communication, accustomed to U.S. client standards, English at professional level.

sections.title: WHY NEAR & WHY NOW
sections.body: Near's model — connecting LatAm talent with high-growth U.S. companies — is exactly the move I've been building toward. Performance marketing demands motion that works, not just motion that looks good. That tension between aesthetics and results is where I'm most at home. Ready for full-time commitment and the pace this role requires.

sections.title: OPENING
sections.body: I'm Felipe Conceição, a motion designer and video editor based in Rio de Janeiro with 8+ years delivering high-velocity content for global brands. I don't just edit — I architect pipelines that compress timelines without compressing quality. When I saw "speed-obsessed" in your brief, I recognized myself.

sections.title: WHAT I BRING
sections.body: I run full post-production pipelines for short-form and long-form simultaneously — Reels, TikToks, YouTube, podcast cuts — using text-based editing in Descript, AI-assisted B-roll via Runway and Kling, and batch repurposing through Opus Clip. A single long-form recording becomes 15+ platform-native clips inside a single session. My NLE stack is Premiere Pro + DaVinci Resolve. Motion work lives in After Effects daily — kinetic type, logo reveals, transitions are standard output, not a bonus skill.

sections.title: WHY DIFFERENT
sections.body: Most editors optimize for quality or speed. I've built workflows that refuse to choose. I approach every project like a systems designer: map the grunt work, automate it, protect creative energy for decisions that actually move the needle. AI tools aren't a shortcut I use reluctantly — they're the first thing I open. That mindset consistently lets me deliver what takes others a full day in a fraction of the time, at a standard clients sign off without revision rounds.

sections.title: WHY DEER & WHY NOW
sections.body: Deer Designer's model — async-first, remote, long-term — matches exactly how I work best: deep focus, high output, no bureaucracy. I'm at a point in my career where I want to embed inside a team that values craft and pace equally, not trade one for the other. This role is that intersection. I'm ready to contribute from day one and grow with the team long-term.

sections.title: OPENING
sections.body: I design how brands move — and how movement builds brands. Eight years translating strategy into motion: broadcast campaigns, rebranding films, motion media kits, and immersive visual systems for names like BMW, Hyundai, Suzano, O Boticário, and Colcci. My background is in motion design and post-production, but my thinking is always in brand.

sections.title: WHAT I BRING
sections.body: Advanced command of After Effects, Premiere, DaVinci Resolve, and Blender — combined with an active AI pipeline using Runway, Kling, Veo3, and Seedance in real production workflows. I work sound-on and sound-off, short-form and long-form, social-first and broadcast-grade. I've led creative output, managed direct reports, and presented work directly to clients and stakeholders across Brazil and internationally.

sections.title: WHY DIFFERENT
sections.body: My foundation is music. As a producer, I think in rhythm, pacing, and silence — which means my motion work isn't just visually considered, it's temporally precise. I don't animate elements. I choreograph communication. That instinct, combined with systems thinking and AI fluency, lets me move fast without losing craft.

sections.title: WHY PENS & WHY NOW
sections.body: I've spent years building independently. What I'm looking for now is to bring that range inside a team — with a Global Creative Director to push against, marketing and brand partners to collaborate with, and a volume of work that demands the kind of multi-layered thinking I operate at. Pens.com is scaling creative across 22 countries. That's the right context for what I do.

sections.title: OPENING
sections.body:
Motion is not what I do — it is how I think. Eight years translating brand strategy into rhythm and image have taught me one thing: the best motion work is invisible. The audience feels the pace before they register the craft. That is the standard I hold every frame to.
I am applying as an independent creative professional, bringing a portfolio that spans brand films, high-ticket campaigns, social content, and generative AI pipelines across twelve countries and forty-plus campaigns. I am looking for a team that demands precision and rewards creative leadership.

sections.title: WHAT I BRING
sections.body:
After Effects and Premiere Pro are my native languages. I have spent eight years building modular motion pipelines — systems designed to scale without losing the aesthetic coherence that makes brand work memorable. From 2D animation and kinetic typography to compositing and colour grading in DaVinci Resolve, I handle the full post-production chain with no handoff gaps.
On the technical side: advanced After Effects, Premiere, DaVinci Resolve, Blender, Photoshop, Illustrator, and Ableton for audio work. I extract and work directly from Figma assets, and I manage multi-format delivery (social, web, OOH, broadcast) as standard practice — not an afterthought.
On the strategic side: I have directed motion for brands like Suzano, O Boticário, Colcci, BMW, and Subaru, working embedded within agency and production house teams across Brazil. I understand brand systems, not just single executions. I know how to maintain visual cohesion across a campaign when assets live in ten different formats simultaneously.

sections.title: WHY DIFFERENT
sections.body:
Most motion designers animate. I direct. The distinction matters when a project moves from a single social asset into a full campaign ecosystem. I have built and managed post-production pipelines, coordinated with cross-functional teams (creative, marketing, production), and delivered under broadcast-grade deadlines — not just agency timelines.
My background in music is not a side note — it is the source of the timing instinct that separates technically correct work from work that actually lands. Audio syncing, sound design, and rhythm-first editing are embedded in how I work, not skills I reach for occasionally.
I also operate at the intersection of 2D craft and emerging AI pipelines (Midjourney, Kling, Seedance, Veo3) — which means I can move faster on generative asset creation without compromising the hand-crafted quality of the final edit. Speed and polish are not a trade-off in my workflow.

sections.title: WHY FEUJI & WHY NOW
sections.body:
he brief describes a role for someone who can blend technical proficiency, creativity, and brand awareness to produce work that is engaging, filmic and polished. That is not an aspirational goal for me — it is the standard I have been working at for eight years.
I am at a point in my career where I want to direct motion inside a growing creative team, not just deliver isolated executions. The collaboration model this role describes — working across creative, marketing, and production — is the environment where I do my best work. I build systems, I communicate clearly in English, and I am self-directed enough to manage multiple projects without needing micromanagement.
This is the right moment for both sides. You need someone who can operate at senior level from day one. I am ready to do exactly that.

sections.title: OPENING
sections.body: Eight years editing to rhythm taught me one thing: the best cut is the one the audience never sees — they just feel it. Short-form news content lives or dies by that instinct. I've spent my career building systems that deliver volume without losing impact, and I follow global current events closely — not just as a habit, but as creative fuel. When I saw this role, I recognized the workflow immediately.

sections.title: WHAT I BRING
sections.body: My background spans broadcast post-production, social content, and AI-assisted pipelines — exactly the stack this role demands. At Rockset I owned post-production for broadcast and digital signage under tight deadlines. At Pelimotion I built modular systems to maintain output volume across formats without sacrificing quality. I'm proficient in Premiere Pro and After Effects, and already use AI tools (Kling, Veo3, Seedance) to accelerate clip generation and captioning. High volume, fast turnaround, strong output — that's not a challenge for me. That's my default mode.

sections.title: WHY DIFFERENT
sections.body: Most editors optimize for polish. I optimize for retention. I think in hooks, pace, and emotional arc — and I do it fast. My music background gave me an internal clock for timing cuts that most technical profiles develop only after years of trial and error. I'm also adaptable by design: I've delivered for automotive, fashion, finance, retail, and live events. News content is a new format, not a new skill set — I find the best solution to maintain impact at the volume required, whatever the brief.

sections.title: WHY SCALE ARMY
sections.body: I'm looking for a remote contract role where speed and creativity aren't in conflict — they're the same thing. A team producing timely, high-impact content around current events is exactly where my mix of editorial instinct, technical range, and AI fluency adds real, immediate value. I work U.S. business hours without friction. I'm ready to deliver from day one.

sections.title: OPENING
sections.body: I'm a motion designer and creative director based in Rio de Janeiro, with eight years producing video content and brand assets across agency, broadcast, and direct-response environments. I'm applying for the Multi-Media Content Producer role because the work you're describing — content that moves people toward action — is precisely the discipline I've built my practice around.

sections.title: WHAT I BRING
sections.body: My core stack is Adobe Premiere Pro, After Effects, and DaVinci Resolve, applied across a wide range of formats: podcast production, digital ad campaigns, social media content, motion graphics, and print-ready design assets. I've worked inside agency pipelines managing multiple client briefs simultaneously, delivering on-brand, on-deadline across digital and physical formats. Recent projects include post-production for a university advertising campaign (FURB), corporate video elevated to broadcast standard for Suzano, and a full motion media kit for an events brand deployed across digital, OOH, and live projection.

sections.title: WHY DIFFERENT
sections.body: Beyond execution, I approach every project from a strategic angle — asking what the content needs to do before deciding how it should look. In a fundraising context, that distinction matters: donor-facing content has to earn attention and trust in the first few seconds. I'm comfortable working with that constraint, and I understand that design serves the mission, not the other way around.

sections.title: WHY BUILDGOOD
sections.body: The model you've built — agency-level expertise applied to the nonprofit sector at scale — is genuinely rare. The opportunity to contribute to campaigns that fund meaningful work, across a variety of organizations and causes, is a strong pull. The part-time, remote, and Latin America-based structure fits my current setup exactly. I'm available to start promptly and work independently from day one.
